title: centaurea subulosa
intro: Centaurea subulosa, also known as Spurred Cabbage or Spurred Thistle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its narrow, grass-like leaves and spiny, thistle-like flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Centaurea subulosa include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in wound healing and skin conditions. Centaurea subulosa is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
